task_completion.description: The agent identified the superior and inferior landmarks of the abdominal aorta.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Control points (nodes) are placed at the aortic hiatus (top of the abdominal aorta) and the aortic bifurcation (where it splits into iliac arteries) in the 2D slice views or 3D view.

task_completion.description: The agent created an open curve markup along the centerline of the aorta.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An open curve markup with multiple control points is visible inside the aorta, following its natural curved path from the hiatus to the bifurcation.

task_completion.description: The agent created a straight-line ruler between the superior and inferior landmarks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A straight line markup (ruler) directly connecting the aortic hiatus and aortic bifurcation is visible in the viewer.

task_completion.description: The agent saved the curve and straight-line markups to the specified files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' dialog in 3D Slicer, a terminal, or file explorer shows 'aortic_curve.mrk.json' and 'aortic_straight.mrk.json' being saved to the '~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/' directory.

task_completion.description: The agent obtained the path length and straight-line distance measurements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Markups module in 3D Slicer or the 3D viewer displays the calculated length of the open curve and the straight line in millimeters.

task_completion.description: The agent computed the tortuosity ratio and saved the final JSON report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or Python console shows the creation or content of 'evar_measurements.json' in the correct directory, containing the fields: path_length_mm, straight_length_mm, tortuosity_ratio, superior_landmark, and inferior_landmark.

integrity.description: The agent used the 3D Slicer GUI to place markups and measure lengths.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 3D Slicer interface with the CT scan loaded and active markups being placed or adjusted manually or via the Python interactor, rather than just writing a script that mocks the values.

integrity.description: The measurements in the report correspond to the values seen in the Slicer GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The length values entered into the final JSON report match the lengths displayed in the Slicer Markups module or 3D viewer.

integrity.description: The agent did not fabricate the report without opening the CT scan.
integrity.visual_evidence: The abdominal CT scan is clearly loaded and visible in the Slicer views during the trajectory.
